methods.description: Analysis of daily Corylus avellana pollen concentrations to identify key dates and define the pollen season.

steps.description: Daily Corylus avellana pollen concentrations in the years 2009 and 2010 in Warsaw were analyzed.Pollen data came from the Allergen Research Center and the Military Medical Institute.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain daily pollen concentration data for Corylus avellana for specified years and location.

steps.description: The start and end dates of the pollen season and days with maximum pollen concentration values were investigated.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify the start date, end date, and dates of maximum values within the daily pollen concentration data.

steps.description: The pollen season was defined as the period when 95% of annual total pollen grains appeared in the air.
steps.searchable_summary: Define the pollen season period based on the cumulative sum reaching 95% of the annual total pollen count.

methods.description: Analysis of Corylus avellana phenology data, focusing on the F2 (first bloom) and F3 (beginning of full bloom) phases, to identify key dates and phase lengths.

steps.description: Phenology data were taken from the Institute of Meteorology and Water Management database that contains information on the generative development of plants, observed using Łukasiewicz's method in 7 stages... The data relating to F2 and F3 phenophases of Corylus avellana in Warsaw in the years 2009 and 2010 were used in the analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain Corylus avellana phenology data for F2 and F3 phases for specified years and location.

steps.description: We defined the start and end dates of the phenophases as well as their length.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify the start date, end date, and calculate the duration for the F2 and F3 phenophases.

methods.description: Use of temperature data to estimate influence on phenology and pollen release, including calculation of temperature anomalies and Positive Degree Days (PDD).

steps.description: Meteorological data -mean monthly temperature, average daily temperature from January to April in 2009 and 2010 in Warsaw -were used...
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain mean monthly temperature and average daily temperature data for specified months, years, and location.

steps.description: Mean monthly temperatures were used to estimate monthly anomalies compared to 1971-2000 normal.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate mean monthly temperature anomalies relative to a historical normal period (1971-2000).

steps.description: By taking the sum of average daily temperatures above 0 o C (from the 1th of January to the day before the F2 phenophase date), we came up with a number called Positive Degree Days (PDD).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Positive Degree Days (PDD) by summing average daily temperatures above 0°C from January 1st up to the day before the F2 start date.

methods.description: Comparing dates of maximum pollen concentration with F2 and F3 phenophases and investigating the relationship between PDD and the start of F2.

steps.description: Dates of maximum pollen concentration during the phenophases F2 and F3 were compared in time.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare the dates of maximum pollen concentration with the time periods of the F2 and F3 phenophases.

steps.description: We tried to check the specific PDD of which could cause the blossoming of the first flowers (F2) in the study years.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine the specific Positive Degree Day (PDD) value that corresponds to the start date of the F2 phenophase.
